author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #597. That issue lowered \`masked_sum\` and \`masked_mean\` to GPU via \`mul(data, mask_f) → reduce_sum\`. \`masked_min\` and \`masked_max\` need a different lowering and a missing primitive.

## Lowering
Idea: replace masked-out entries with sentinel values that don't change the extremum, then reduce.

\`\`\`text
masked_min(data, mask) = reduce_min(where(mask, data, +∞))
masked_max(data, mask) = reduce_max(where(mask, data, -∞))
\`\`\`

The all-masked case lands on the sentinel value (+∞ for min, -∞ for max); the existing CPU path returns NaN in that case, so the GPU path either:
1. host-checks \`count_valid == 0\` like \`masked_mean_gpu\` already does and short-circuits to NaN, or
2. detects the sentinel afterwards and re-emits NaN.

Option 1 is simpler — \`count_valid\` is computed cheaply from the bool mask on host.

## Missing primitive
\`reduce_min\` / \`reduce_max\` aren't in \`ferrotorch-gpu/src/kernels.rs\` yet. The existing \`reduce_sum\` PTX kernel is the model — the same two-pass block reduction with the operator and identity element swapped. Need both f32 and f64 variants.

## Implementation outline
1. Add PTX \`REDUCE_MIN_PTX\` / \`REDUCE_MAX_PTX\` kernels modeled on \`REDUCE_SUM_PTX\` (\`min\`/\`max\` instead of \`add\`, \`+inf\`/\`-inf\` initial accumulator).
2. Wire them through the f32 path in \`gpu_reduce_min\` / \`gpu_reduce_max\` and the f64 path via the existing \`get_f64_ptx\` rewriter (note: the rewriter handles the \`+inf\`/\`-inf\` hex literal substitutions already — see #575 for the offset-shift fix that made f64 reductions work).
3. Add trait methods \`min_f32/f64\` / \`max_f32/f64\` to \`GpuBackend\`, \`CudaBackendImpl\` impls, then mirror the existing \`masked_sum_gpu\` / \`masked_mean_gpu\` lowering in \`masked.rs\`.

## Where filling happens
For the inf-fill: \`mask_as_float_tensor\` in \`masked.rs\` already builds a 0/1 mask. Need a new helper that builds a tensor of sentinels at masked positions: \`fill = (1 - mask) * sentinel\`. Then \`combined = data * mask + fill\` is what gets reduced.
